ucs.title: [LLM] Turla ComRAT v4 / PowerStallion cloud-service C2 (Gmail + OneDrive dead-drops) from non-browser process
ucs.description: Hunts Turla's signature post-2019 tradecraft of using legitimate consumer cloud services (Gmail web UI for ComRAT v4 tasking; OneDrive for the PowerStallion PowerShell backdoor) as a covert C2 channel. We surface PowerShell, rundll32, svchost children and other non-browser binaries that establish HTTPS sessions to mail.google.com / googleusercontent / OneDrive endpoints — a behavioural pattern documented by ESET and Microsoft (Secret Blizzard) for ComRAT v4 and PowerStallion.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port dc(_time) as conn_minutes from datamodel=Network_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest IN ("mail.google.com","www.googleapis.com","mail-attachment.googleusercontent.com","gmail.com","outlook.live.com","onedrive.live.com","api.onedrive.com","graph.microsoft.com") OR All_Traffic.dest="*.googleusercontent.com") AND NOT (All_Traffic.process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","arc.exe","outlook.exe","onedrive.exe","teams.exe","ms-teams.exe","explorer.exe","searchapp.exe","officeclicktorun.exe","msoia.exe","onedrivesetup.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe")) by host All_Traffic.process_name All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | where count>=3 AND NOT match(user,"\$$") | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Turla ComRAT v4 (Gmail) + PowerStallion (OneDrive) cloud-service C2
let CloudC2Hosts = dynamic([
    "mail.google.com","www.googleapis.com","mail-attachment.googleusercontent.com",
    "gmail.com","outlook.live.com","onedrive.live.com","api.onedrive.com",
    "graph.microsoft.com"
]);
let _LegitBrowsers = dynamic([
    "chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","arc.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe",
    "outlook.exe","onedrive.exe","teams.exe","ms-teams.exe","searchapp.exe",
    "explorer.exe","onedrivesetup.exe","msoia.exe","officeclicktorun.exe",
    "backgroundtaskhost.exe","runtimebroker.exe"
]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteUrl in~ (CloudC2Hosts) or RemoteUrl endswith ".googleusercontent.com"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_LegitBrowsers)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1m)),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            RemoteIPs = make_set(RemoteIP, 10)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
               InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemoteUrl
| where ConnCount >= 3                  // 3+ beacons distinguishes C2 from one-off probe
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Turla ComRAT v4 (Gmail) + PowerStallion (OneDrive) cloud-service C2 — ASIM port
let CloudC2Hosts = dynamic([
    "mail.google.com","www.googleapis.com","mail-attachment.googleusercontent.com",
    "gmail.com","outlook.live.com","onedrive.live.com","api.onedrive.com",
    "graph.microsoft.com"
]);
let _LegitBrowsers = dynamic([
    "chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","arc.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe",
    "outlook.exe","onedrive.exe","teams.exe","ms-teams.exe","searchapp.exe",
    "explorer.exe","onedrivesetup.exe","msoia.exe","officeclicktorun.exe",
    "backgroundtaskhost.exe","runtimebroker.exe"
]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where DstHostname in~ (CloudC2Hosts)
   or (isnotempty(Url) and Url has_any (CloudC2Hosts))
   or DstHostname endswith ".googleusercontent.com"
| extend Proc = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where Proc !in~ (_LegitBrowsers)
| where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)),
            SampleCmd = any(ActingProcessCommandLine),
            DstIPs = make_set(DstIpAddr, 10)
            by DvcHostname, Proc, SrcUsername, DstHostname
| where ConnCount >= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Turla ComRAT v4 / PowerStallion Cloud-Service C2 (Non-Browser to Gmail/OneDrive)
id: 7d4f4b0e-9a13-4a31-b1a7-7e3e4f7e9c77
status: experimental
description: Detects non-browser processes (PowerShell, rundll32, svchost descendants) initiating HTTPS sessions to Gmail / OneDrive / Graph endpoints used by Turla's ComRAT v4 (Gmail dead-drop) and PowerStallion (OneDrive C2) backdoors.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0010/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2020/05/26/agent-btz-comrat-v4-ten-year-journey/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2019/05/29/turla-powershell-usage/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/05/09
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_dst:
    DestinationHostname:
      - 'mail.google.com'
      - 'www.googleapis.com'
      - 'mail-attachment.googleusercontent.com'
      - 'gmail.com'
      - 'outlook.live.com'
      - 'onedrive.live.com'
      - 'api.onedrive.com'
      - 'graph.microsoft.com'
  filter_browsers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\iexplore.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\onedrive.exe'
      - '\teams.exe'
      - '\ms-teams.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\searchapp.exe'
      - '\officeclicktorun.exe'
  condition: selection_dst and not filter_browsers
falsepositives:
  - Custom in-house automation that legitimately uses Graph/OneDrive APIs (allowlist by SHA256)
  - Backup utilities authoring to OneDrive
level: high
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.g0010
ucs.rationale: Other APTs use cloud services for exfil, but Turla is uniquely documented (ESET 2019/2020, Microsoft Secret Blizzard 2023) as operationalising Gmail's web UI as a bidirectional C2 channel via stolen cookies (ComRAT v4) AND OneDrive as a dead-drop for a PowerShell backdoor (PowerStallion). The behavioural fingerprint — non-browser binary speaking HTTPS to mail.google.com / api.onedrive.com — is hig

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] Turla Snake/Uroburos kernel rootkit drop into legacy $NtUninstall* paths
ucs.description: Catches Turla's flagship rootkit (Snake / Uroburos / Krypton) installation artefacts. Snake hides its kernel driver, virtual filesystem queue, and configuration inside Windows Update uninstaller folders such as %SystemRoot%\$NtUninstallQ817473$\ and uses very specific driver filenames (fdisk_mbr.sys, usbsndrv.sys, rkhdrv40.sys/rkhdrv32.sys). The May-2023 CISA / NSA / NCSC joint advisory on Snake calls these out as durable IOCs across 20+ years of the toolkit's evolution.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.process_name) as process_name values(Filesystem.user) as user values(Filesystem.action) as action from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name IN ("fdisk_mbr.sys","usbsndrv.sys","rkhdrv40.sys","rkhdrv32.sys","termservice.sys") OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\$NtUninstallQ817473$\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\$NtUninstall*\\*.sys") AND Filesystem.action IN ("created","modified","renamed") by host Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | where NOT process_name IN ("TrustedInstaller.exe","msiexec.exe","TiWorker.exe","WindowsUpdateBox.exe","wuauclt.exe") | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Turla Snake/Uroburos kernel rootkit artefacts
// IOCs: CISA AA23-129A — Snake malware joint advisory
let SnakeFileNames = dynamic([
    "fdisk_mbr.sys","usbsndrv.sys","rkhdrv40.sys","rkhdrv32.sys",
    "termservice.sys"
]);
let SnakeFolderPatterns = dynamic([
    @"\$NtUninstallQ817473$\",
    @"\$NtUninstall"
]);
let _LegitInstallers = dynamic([
    "trustedinstaller.exe","msiexec.exe","tiworker.exe",
    "wuauclt.exe","windowsupdatebox.exe","setuphost.exe"
]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
| where FileName in~ (SnakeFileNames)
   or FolderPath has_any (SnakeFolderPatterns)
   or (FileName endswith ".sys" and FolderPath has @"\$NtUninstall")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_LegitInstallers)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, FileName, FolderPath,
          SHA256, FileSize,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Turla Snake/Uroburos kernel rootkit artefacts — ASIM port
let SnakeFileNames = dynamic([
    "fdisk_mbr.sys","usbsndrv.sys","rkhdrv40.sys","rkhdrv32.sys",
    "termservice.sys"
]);
let SnakeFolderPatterns = dynamic([
    @"\$NtUninstallQ817473$\",
    @"\$NtUninstall"
]);
let _LegitInstallers = dynamic([
    "trustedinstaller.exe","msiexec.exe","tiworker.exe",
    "wuauclt.exe","windowsupdatebox.exe","setuphost.exe"
]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
| extend ProcLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where TargetFileName in~ (SnakeFileNames)
   or TargetFilePath has_any (SnakeFolderPatterns)
   or (TargetFileName endswith ".sys" and TargetFilePath has @"\$NtUninstall")
| where ProcLeaf !in~ (_LegitInstallers)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, EventType, TargetFileName, TargetFilePath,
          TargetFileSHA256, TargetFileSize,
          ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, ActorUsername
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Turla Snake/Uroburos Kernel Driver Drop in Legacy $NtUninstall Path
id: 1f3a8e9c-2b4f-4d7a-9b7c-7f2c2b8e4a55
status: experimental
description: Detects file artefacts uniquely associated with Turla's Snake/Uroburos kernel rootkit — fdisk_mbr.sys / usbsndrv.sys / rkhdrv40.sys / rkhdrv32.sys, or any .sys dropped under %SystemRoot%\$NtUninstall*\ (per CISA AA23-129A IOCs).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0010/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-129a
  - https://www.gov.uk/government/news/uk-and-allies-disclose-evolution-of-snake-malware
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/05/09
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_known_names:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\fdisk_mbr.sys'
      - '\usbsndrv.sys'
      - '\rkhdrv40.sys'
      - '\rkhdrv32.sys'
  selection_path_marker:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\$NtUninstallQ817473$\'
  selection_path_sys:
    TargetFilename|contains: '\$NtUninstall'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.sys'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\TiWorker.exe'
      - '\WindowsUpdateBox.exe'
  condition: (selection_known_names or selection_path_marker or selection_path_sys) and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Forensic / IR tools restoring legacy uninstall folders
  - Pre-Windows 10 systems with original $NtUninstall* directories left in place
level: critical
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1014
  - attack.t1543.003
  - attack.t1564.004
  - attack.g0010
ucs.rationale: These filenames and the $NtUninstallQ817473$ path are not generic — they are Snake-specific IOCs published by CISA, NSA, NCSC and CSE in the May 2023 Snake takedown advisory and have been observed across two decades of Turla operations (Uroburos → Snake). Modern Windows builds do not create $NtUninstall* uninstaller directories, so any .sys file appearing in such a path is a near-zero-FP signal of
